description:
Spec §12 today: 'A push that fails for lack of perms falls back to stealth-local silently.' That silent fallback is only defensible for FOUNDING — the remote store branch is absent and you lack create perms, so 'couldn't found, stayed local' is genuinely harmless and once-per-clone.

It is WRONG for an established remote: an upstream rejection of a push to an already-existing store (non-ff, perms revoked, server hook reject) means your mutation did not land while you believe you are federated — a silent split-brain. Those two cases must not collapse to one silent path.

Resolution: distinguish at the tracker:
- founding-miss (branch absent, no create perm) -> stealth fallback, as today (optionally a notice).
- rejected push to an established remote -> ERROR (surface it; do not silently degrade to stealth).

Touches §12, §15 (revision-log entry).
title: Greenfield §12: split prime push-failure — founding-miss -> stealth fallback; rejected push to established remote -> error
